title: The One Where They All Turn Thirty

lines.text: Joey and Rachel's, Joey is knocking on Rachel's door, whose door frame is decorated with balloons. The rest of the gang is there as well. Rachel opens the door and the gang blow on noisemakers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Happy birthday!!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Happy birthday!!!
Rachel glares at them and goes back into her room, closing her door.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Rach! Come on! Rach!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's your birthday!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: (entering from her room) Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) She's not as pretty as she was when she was 29.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Ms. Green would like to establish some ground rules before she comes out. She would appreciate it if you don't use the words old or downhill or (To Joey) they still look pretty damn good. (Joey smiles and everyone glares at him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They do!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel! Come on out! Monica made breakfast!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chocolate-chip pancakes!
There is no response from Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We've got presents!
She opens the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good ones?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They all came from the list you handed out to us two weeks ago.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, can I keep the presents and still be 29?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on Rach! Look, turning thirty is not that big a deal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh really. Is that how you felt when you turned thirty?
[Flashback to Joey's thirtieth birthday party. It is being held in Monica and Chandler's apartment.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (screaming) Why God?!! Why?!! We had a deal!! Let the others grow old! Not me!! (He buries his head in Phoebe's lap for comfort.)
[Cut back to Rachel's party, everyone is now eating breakfast, except Rachel.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, I'm still 29 in Guam.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, 30 is not that old! Do you know how old the Earth is?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Late thirties? Oh come on you guys! Is it just me? Am I overreacting to this?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No Rach, it's not just you. My thirtieth birthday certainly wasn't that much fun.
[Flashback to Chandler's thirtieth birthday party. It is also being held in Monica and his apartment. He is about to blow out the candles on his birthday cake.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (screaming) And now Chandler! We're all gettin' so old! (Looking up) Why are you doing this to us?! (Turns away crying.)
Opening Credits
Joey and Rachel's, breakfast is finished but Rachel's still down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach, you're in a great place in your life. Come on, you've got a great job! Good friends...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you're roommate is a soap opera star.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, y'know I know my life's going pretty well, but I look around and I just see so many people who've accomplished so many other goals by the time they're thirty.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but you shouldn't compare yourself to me.
[Flashback to: The Street in front of Central Perk, Ross and Joey are holding a yellow tape across the road and everyone is cheering Phoebe as she bounces around the corner on a hippity-hop.]

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Come on Phoebe! You can do it Phoebe! Come on!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: There you go!
She crosses the line and they all cheer again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I did it! One mile on a hippity-hop! That's it!! That's everything I wanted to do before I was thirty. Oh, except I wanted to patch things up with my sister. But oh well. Yay!! (They all cheer again.) And-and girls this thing is a Godsend if you know what I mean. (Rachel and Monica look intrigued and as they all head into Central Perk, Rachel picks up the hippity-hop that Phoebe left behind.)
Joey and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thirty. Ugh, I mean thirty! Monica, do you remember mean, old Mrs. Kreeger in the fifth grade? She was thirty!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Come on, let's have some fun. Huh? (To Rachel) What do you want to do today?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing. I don't want to do anything.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, doing nothing on your thirtieth is better than doing something stupid, like Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! That was a practical purchase! I needed that car for transportation! Okay? I-I have a child!

[Flashback to: A street, Ross is sitting in his newly purchased MGB. Which is one of the better British sports cars ever made. Of course, 'better' is a relative term. Which reminds me of a joke. Why don't the British make computers? Because they couldn't figure out how to make them leak oil. Anyway, the gang is all staring at his new purchase.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How hot do I look in this, huh?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross, a sports car? Wouldn't it have been cheaper to just stuff a sock down there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's not what this is about okay? I-I am a sports car enthusiast. I have always been into cars.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what's the horsepower on this thing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (giddy) I don't know, but-but look how shiny!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe you bought this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really! God Ross, what were you thinking? (To Phoebe, quietly) I know it's really shallow, but a part of me wants him again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, well get in line missy. (To Ross) So, can I have a ride stud?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hop in. (Phoebe hops in.) Get ready for the smoothest ride of your life.

He starts the car and surprisingly in fires right up and comes to a nice idle. (Both can be rarities with British sports cars with their lovely Lucas ignition systems, which tend not to work especially in the rain.) Anyway, this being New York he is parallel parked on a street with the car in front of him only inches ahead of his bumper, likewise with the car behind him. He's completely boxed in and can't move more than two inches. He tries to get out several times by bumping the bumpers of both cars to no avail.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damnit! (Shuts the car off.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (getting out) Okay, who's next?
Joey and Rachel's, Phoebe is pouring Rachel some coffee.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know what? I am going to do something today. I'm not just gonna sit around like some old lady. I'm gonna get something pierced. Like my uh, like my nose or my tongue or something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?! 'Cause y'know that hurts.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So what?! Y'know what? The way I see it-(Phoebe pulls out a hair from the back of her head)-Ow! Son of a bitch!!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Look Rachel, I know what you're going through. I'm totally freaked about turning 25.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (glares at him) Get out, get out of my apartment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right Rach, for what it's worth, I think that you're doing great. I mean y'know let's face it, no one handles this well.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Least of all you.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Why? What you'd do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Weren't you asked to leave sonny?
[Flashback to Monica and Chandler's apartment. Chandler has a bunch of people over in formal wear to give Monica a surprise birthday party. Joey is coming out of the bathroom and removing his tie.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Joey) Would you put that back on?! Monica's gonna be here any minute!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But it hurt's my Joey's Apple.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (frustrated) Okay, for the last time. It's not named for each individual man.
Joey walks away and Mr. and Mrs. Geller walk up. Mr. Geller is wearing this ancient velvet tuxedo.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (To Chandler) You've done a wonderful job with this party Chandler. Everything looks so lovely.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh well, not as lovely as you. I mean, I can't believe that you would have a thirty-year-old daughter! (To Mr. Geller) And you! I can't believe that you would have a tux that's thirty years old! (Puts his hand on Mr. Geller's shoulder.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: It's older than that. Ross was actually conceived right near this tuxedo.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh! (He quickly removes his hand and looks at it.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey! Everybody hide! Hide! I saw her! She's coming!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! Okay! Everybody down! Everybody down! (Rachel turns off the lights and everyone crouches. As everyone crouches, a ripping noise erupts from the assemblage.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Crap.
We hear some fumbling at the door, then silence.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (getting up to investigate) Okay, everybody stay here. I will find out what's going on.
He goes out into the hall and finds a very drunk Monica lying up against Joey and Rachel's door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Heyyy!! You got the door open!! (Giggles.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey-hey are you drunk?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nooo! (Giggles) Okay. (She tries to pull herself up by Rachel and Joey's doorknob, but the door opens and she almost falls into the their apartment. She manages to catch herself.) Whoa! (Stands up, unsteadily) Okay. See I was, I was a little nervous about turning (whispering) thirty. (Giggles.) So the bus boys took me out for some drinks. (Pause) I wanna puke on you later!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here is the thing. We have thrown a very formal surprise party for you in there! All of your friends are in there and your parents!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Noo!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Noo!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no! My parents have never seen me drunk! (Pause) That they know of.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here's the thing. We're gonna get you some coffee and they will never know that you're drunk.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?! You promise?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I'll take care of it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I love you so much. (Kisses him.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (laughing) Okay we have to do something about your breath.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about your breath?! (Breathes on him.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's still yours. Okay, now remember it's a surprise party. So, when you go in, act surprised.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I can do that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
Chandler opens the door and Monica sneaks up on it. They go inside.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Surprise!!!
Monica screams and they all stare at her.
Commercial Break
Ross's birthday, Joey is now trying to get his car out while Ross is directing him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, forward. Forward-Stop! (The car moves an inch and Ross runs to the back of the car.) Okay, back-Stop! (The car barely moves and Ross runs back to the front.) Okay, forward-Stop! Stop! Stop!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, just forget about it. This guy's got you totally wedged in.
A beautiful woman approaches.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: (To Joey) Is this yours?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well actually...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no-no! It's mine! It's-it's mine. (The woman walks away.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, you soooo need this car.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (running up) Okay. Okay, here's what we're gonna do. Okay, I'm gonna break into this mini-van and put it in neutral. You guys push it forward so Ross can drive out of his spot. Okay? All right, here we go. (She opens her coat and reveals that thing car thieves use to break into cars as Ross jumps in behind the wheel. She inserts the device, unlocks the door, opens it, and the alarm goes off.) Haul ass!!!! (Runs off.)
The rest of the gang runs away, except for Ross who's tramped inside his car. To hide he puts the top up as Monica, Rachel, and Joey come running past.
Phoebe's birthday, she's taking the hippity-hop to Ursula's apartment as a gift. She knocks on the door and Ursula answers it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Happy thirtieth birthday! Here! (Hands her the hippity-hop.) It's for the child in you, and the woman. Happy thirtieth!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Right, why do you keep saying that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because it's our thirtieth birthday.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, no we're not thirty. We're 31. Okay. (She closes the door.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait! (Knocks on the door and Ursula opens it.)

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Oh, it's you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. What?!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, we're not thirty, we're 31.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nu-uh!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yea-huh! That's what is says on my birth certificate.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You have your birth certificate?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, I got a big box of family stuff when my mom died.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Our mom.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: (sarcastic) Right! Okay. (Hands Phoebe her births certificate.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do you have my birth certificate?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: No, I sold it to a Swedish runaway.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (reading the certificate) Oh my God! Oh my God, we are 31.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just lost a whole year of my life.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: (sarcastic) Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Your middle name is Pamela?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I never knew mine. Do you remember what it is?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yes! Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's my first name.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Right, okay, then no.
Monica's birthday, it's just after the surprise.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay before we start the celebration, Monica has to go put on her party dress.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See? (Does his laugh.) Here we go. (Starts walking her to their room, and has to pass in front of Mr. Geller who's sitting at the table and Mrs. Geller who's standing next to him.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Happy birthday, sweetie! Give us a hug! (Starts to get up.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (stopping him) Don't get up Jack! The safety pins are about to blow.
They continue their trek.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sees someone) Paul!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (correcting her) Phil.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phil!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now, there is a dress laid out on your bed. (Monica stumbles on the steps.) Okay, (catches her) you're doing great. You're doing great. You're doing fine.
Phoebe approaches as they almost get to their room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica's a little drunk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay! I love drunk Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Awwwww... (Giggles.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Go change! (To Phoebe) She doesn't want her parents to know she's drunk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh! All right! All right. Here's what we'll do, I'll get twice as drunk as Monica and then no one's will even notice her.
Chandler walks over to where the rest of the gang is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's-what's going on? Phil's really pissed!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica's wasted.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe that will liven up this party.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Rachel) Okay, will you just go help her change please!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, but taking care of a drunk, naked woman seems like a job for Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yep. (Starts for Monica's room, but Chandler stops him.)
Rachel's birthday, everyone is presenting their presents to Rachel.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: (handing his to her) This one's from me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahh!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: It wasn't on your list, but hopefully you'll think it's really fun.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (opening it) A scooter! (She's not happy.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Tag) Stick to the list. Always stick to the list.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No-no, I love it. Thank you. (Kisses him.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, open ours next. Open ours next!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now that you're a couple, we don't get two presents from you guys?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: For my last birthday you gave me a hug! (To Rachel) Okay, read the card! Read the card!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (Opens the card and reads it.) Happy birthday Grandma! It's better to be over the hill (starting to cry) then buried under it. (Breaks down as everyone glares at them.) All our love Monica and Chandler. (Crying) That's funny, yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no! That was the joke!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (crying) No, I know! I get it! It's funny!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, because you're not a grandmother!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No I know, because to be a grandmother you have to be married and have children and I don't have any of those things. That's why it's so funny. (Runs into her room crying.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All you had to do was buy the card!
Rachel's birthday, a time lapse has occurred. Rachel is coming back into the living room carrying a notepad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Look who's back! It's the birthday girl! How's the birthday girl feeling?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I feel fine, but I think you're bumming out the rest of the kids.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? (Glances over and sees the faces of the rest of the group, then goes and sits down.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! Y'know what? I realized it was stupid to get upset about not having a husband and kids. All I really needed was a plan. See I wanna have three kids...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh let me guess, and you wanna have them all at the same time and you wanna have them for your brother.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: As I was saying... I should probably have the first of the three kids by the time I'm 35 which gives me five years. I love this plan! I wanna marry this plan!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: If you could do that, I'd marry the hippity-hop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, if I wanna have my kid when I'm 35, I don't have to get pregnant until I'm 34. Which gives Prada four years to start making maternity clothes! Oh wait, but I do want to be married for a year before I get pregnant...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really! That long?! (Chandler slowly turns and looks at her.) (To Chandler) Look all you want, it's happening!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, so I don't have to get married until I'm 33! That's three years, that's three whole years-Oh, wait a minute though. I'll need a year and a half to plan the wedding, and I'd like to know the guy for a year, year and a half before we get engaged... Which means I need to meet the guy by the time I'm thirty.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Which is fine! Because you just turned-(Removes two candles from the cake)-twenty-eight!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Ross, no! It is not fine! Eh-eh-according to my plan I should already be with the guy I wanna marry!
We hear Tag scream out in the hallway and jump into view of the open door on the scooter. He gives a hearty thumbs up to the group and rides off, with Joey following breathlessly behind.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Will you quit hoggin' it!
Commercial Break
Monica's birthday, Monica is now dressed and is being helped out by Chandler and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Chandler) I'm telling you it's like watching Bambi learn how to walk.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Monica) You're drunk! Mom and dad are gonna be maaaaadd! Maybe I'm a little drunk.
Monica sits down on the barca lounger.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to a waiter) Oh that's great! Right there! Can we get some of that over here please? (The waiter comes over) There we go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to the waiter) Hey! Are those crab cakes? (The waiter nods) Did I not tell ya to come straight to me when more crab cakes were ready?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You are so handsome! I wanna make love to you right here, right now! (Growls and pulls him into a kiss.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I really wish that you wouldn't.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Now all you have to do is just get through a little bit more, okay? Then we can put you in bed, okay? Just smile and don't talk to anyone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (clinking two glasses together) Speech! Speech! Let's hear from the birthday girl! Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't you see? Everyone's looking at me! The plan's working! I didn't even have to take off my top yet!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Speech! Come on Monica!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Come on! Speech!
Monica stands up and wobbles slightly and Chandler runs over to catch her.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (filming this) Hey Chandler, you can't keep your hands off her for one second!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh-ho, I think it's nice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think it's necessary. (Backs away anyhow.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I-I-I wanna thank you all for coming. My family and my friends...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (screaming) Wooo!! Hoo!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I really like to say that I'm-um... (Pause) Y'know what I'd really like to say? I'm drunk!! (Mrs. Geller pulls the camera down.) That's right mom and dad your little Harmonica is hammered!! (Ross grabs the camera out of his dad's hands.) And guess what! I've been drunk before! And I've smoked a cigarette! And I got a box of Ding-Dongs hidden in my underwear drawer! It's all okay. It's okay because I turned thirty today. And, and I can do anything I want! Because I am a grown up. (Falls over sideways with Ross filming the fall.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Joey) Okay quick, help me get this off! (Motions to her top.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!!
Ross pans the camera over to Phoebe.
Phoebe's birthday, she's telling everyone what she found out at Ursula's while sitting in Central Perk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I lost a whole year! I can't believe it! This is so unfair!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I don't know Pheebs. It'll be okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Will it? Will it?! I mean, how would you feel if you found out you were 31?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's not gonna happen. No. (Looks up) Because we have a new deal!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Plus, it totally ruined my schedule! I...I haven't done any of the things I wanted to do by the time I was 31!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Like okay I-I-I, I haven't met any Portuguese people! I, I haven't had the perfect kiss! And I haven't been to sniper's school!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, y'know why don't we just go upstairs and have some birthday cake?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I just feel like being by myself for a while. All right? I'll see you guys later. Thanks. (Gets up and exits.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey. (After she leaves.) Oh, poor Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, y'know what you guys? I think I'm gonna go walk her home. (Gets up and runs out.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh man!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He's gonna eat the cake!
[Cut outside, Joey is catching up with Phoebe.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs! Wait up! (She stops.) Listen uh, close your eyes. (She does so and Joey passionately kisses her.) Maybe that's one thing you can cross off your list.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah.
Joey starts to walk away, but stops.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, and plus I'm 1/16th Portuguese.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! (Phoebe walks away smiling.)
Ross's birthday, his car is still trapped in it's spot. Now Joey, Phoebe, and Ross are at the front of the car with Monica, Rachel, and Chandler at the rear of the car.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, is everybody clear? We're gonna pick it up...and move it. Now all we need is teamwork, okay? We're gonna lift the car...and slide it out. Lift and slide!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I really don't think...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (interrupting her) Lift!! And slide!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here we go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right everyone, lift! (They and try to lift the car, of course it doesn't raise up) And slide!! (Everyone leans over, but the car still does not move.)
Rachel's birthday, she is coming into the hallway where Joey and Tag are playing with the scooter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Joey, can I...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, come on Rach! My turn just started!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Actually, I just wanna talk to Tag.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. Okay. Hey, can I ride this outside?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whatever! Okay, I'm not your mother.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay! (Runs off downstairs.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Not in the street!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Tag) Hi.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: How are you doing? Are you feeling any better?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I'm doing okay. I'm um...let's talk.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Okay. (They sit on the step.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm...

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What's up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh Tag, umm...you're such a great guy and we have sooo much fun together but I don't-I don't...

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Wait! I think I see where you're going, but before you say anything else, can I just say one more thing? (Kisses her.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well said. And a uh good example of the fun I was referring to uhh, but I just think I'm past the point where I think I can y'know, just have fun.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Rachel, don't do this. This is just because you're turning thirty.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it is! But you're just a kid! I mean you're 25!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Twenty-four actually.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God! Y'know what I wish? I wish you were six years older. Well actually, if I'm wishin' for stuff, I actually wish I was six years younger.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I'm sorry. (They hug.)
[Time lapse, Rachel is entering her apartment after breaking up with Tag.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! How'd it go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, if I only want two kids, can I keep him for another year?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You did the right thing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering, limping, and holding his arm) I don't like this anymore. (He sits down with them in pain.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, here we are, just a bunch of thirty year olds.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: God, do you realize in ten years we're gonna be 40?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (crying) Why God?! Why are you doing this to us?! (He buries his head in Ross's shoulder.)
Ross's birthday, night has fallen and Joey and Ross are walking by where his car is parked to find that both cars blocking him in have left.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! My baby's finally free!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All-all right! (They run and jump in the car.) Start it up! Let's go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (starting it) Woohoo!
Just as they are about to pull away, a big, fat, bald guy pulls up in the exact same car as Ross and stops next to him.

lines.speaker: The Man In The Sportscar
lines.text: How hot are we? (He drives off.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You wanna buy a car?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.
Ross shuts it off and they get out.
Ending Credits
{Transcriber's Note: There was no credits scene with this episode.}
End
